subtaskTitle: Check for habits of swearing by other than Allah

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Whoever swears by other than Allah has committed shirk" (Ahmad, Tirmidhi).

resolved.note: While Sahih al-Bukhari (844) and Sahih Muslim (Book 1, Chapter 28) record prohibitions against swearing by other than Allah, the specific wording regarding 'shirk' and the attribution to Ahmad/Tirmidhi are not present in the provided sources.

subtaskTitle: Study the conditions for declaring takfir and why it requires caution

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) warned that calling a Muslim a disbeliever unjustly returns the accusation upon the accuser.

resolved.note: The source states: 'When a man calls his brother an unbeliever, it returns (at least) to one of them.'

subtaskTitle: Pray each salah within its earliest time window

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) was asked which deed is best and said: "Prayer at its proper time" (Bukhari/Muslim).

resolved.note: The Prophet was asked which deed is 'dearest to Allah' and replied: 'To offer the prayers at their early stated fixed times.' Also recorded in Sahih Muslim 151.

subtaskTitle: Practice performing wudu with correct sequence and du'a

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) said that whoever performs wudu then recites the Shahada, the eight gates of Jannah are opened for them (Muslim).

resolved.note: States that for whoever performs wudu well and recites the Shahada, 'the eight gates of Paradise would be opened for him.'

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) said the two rak'at before Fajr are "better than the world and everything in it" (Muslim).

resolved.note: Recorded as: 'The two rak'ahs at dawn are better than this world and what it contains.'

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) was consistent with the Dhuhr Sunnah prayers and said: "Whoever is regular with four rak'at before Dhuhr and four after, the Fire will not touch him" (Tirmidhi).

resolved.note: Although Sahih al-Bukhari (275) notes the Prophet never missed four rak'at before Dhuhr, the specific promise that 'the Fire will not touch him' attributed to Tirmidhi is not in the provided sources.

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) said: "Whoever prays twelve rak'at during the day and night, a house will be built for him in Jannah" (Muslim).

resolved.note: States: 'A house will be built in Paradise, for anyone who prays in a day and a night twelve rak'ahs.'

subtaskTitle: Make du'a during the sujud of Tahajjud

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The closest a servant is to his Lord is when he is in sujud, so increase your du'a therein" (Muslim).

resolved.note: While the sources discuss the excellence of sujud and prayers therein (e.g., Sahih Muslim 980), the exact quote regarding a servant being 'closest' to his Lord in sujud is not found in the provided excerpts.

subtaskTitle: Pray Duha at least 3 times this week

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) advised Abu Hurayrah (RA) to never abandon Duha prayer.

resolved.note: Abu Huraira reported that his friend (the Prophet) instructed him in three things, including 'two rak'ahs of the forenoon prayer' (Duha). Also in Sahih al-Bukhari 202.

subtaskTitle: Choose a consistent time in the last third of the night

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) said: "The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small" (Bukhari).

resolved.note: States: 'the best deed (act of Worship) in the sight of Allah is that which is done regularly.' Also found in Sahih Muslim 1710.

subtaskTitle: Make it a consistent monthly practice

payload.sentence: Abu Hurayrah (RA) reported that the Prophet (SAW) advised him to fast three days of every month (Bukhari/Muslim).

resolved.note: Abu Huraira reported the Prophet advised him 'to fast three days a month.' Also recorded in Sahih Muslim 1558.

subtaskTitle: Practice delaying suhoor and hastening iftar as Sunnah

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) said his ummah will remain upon goodness as long as they hasten the iftar and delay the suhoor (Ahmad).

resolved.note: The provided sources mention 'hastening iftar' and the blessing of 'suhoor' (Sahih Muslim 2412, 2420), but the combined quote regarding the ummah remaining upon goodness is not present.

subtaskTitle: Fast the day of Arafah (for non-pilgrims)

payload.sentence: The Prophet (SAW) said fasting the day of Arafah expiates the sins of the previous year and the coming year (Muslim).

resolved.note: The hadith states: 'I seek from Allah that fasting on the day of 'Arafa may atone for the sins of the preceding and the coming years.'
